title: podman is looking for qemu 8.0.0 but depends on 8.0.2
body:
### Issue Description

I'm not exactly sure how I got into this state on my machine, I think I reinstalled podman via brew. After, I tried to run a podman command and got an error. I tried restarting the podman machine and got the following error:

```
podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Waiting for VM ...
Error: qemu exited unexpectedly with exit code 1, stderr: qemu-system-aarch64: -drive file=/opt/homebrew/Cellar/qemu/8.0.0/share/qemu/edk2-aarch64-code.fd,if=pflash,format=raw,readonly=on: Could not open '/opt/homebrew/Cellar/qemu/8.0.0/share/qemu/edk2-aarch64-code.fd': No such file or directory
```

I have qemu 8.0.2 on my machine, but it looks like this command wants 8.0.0 which I was unable to install.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. On an m2 mac, install the latest podman via brew
2. Init and start a machine
3. See error


### Describe the results you received

```
podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Waiting for VM ...
Error: qemu exited unexpectedly with exit code 1, stderr: qemu-system-aarch64: -drive file=/opt/homebrew/Cellar/qemu/8.0.0/share/qemu/edk2-aarch64-code.fd,if=pflash,format=raw,readonly=on: Could not open '/opt/homebrew/Cellar/qemu/8.0.0/share/qemu/edk2-aarch64-code.fd': No such file or directory
```

### Describe the results you expected

podman commands work as expected

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.
`Error: failed to connect: dial tcp [::1]:64320: connect: connection refused`

podman version: 4.5.1
OS: MacBook Air Apple M2 Ventura 13.4.1
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

To be honest I have not touched podman on this machine in awhile and just before trying to run podman today I was trying to get ruby 3.1.2 to compile which was also failing, so maybe it's just a me problem!

comments.body: Worked around this by starting a new machine and switching the connection to it. I suppose this is expected if the needed qemu version changes.
